strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|relative strength was clearly supportive|news/catalyst quality was mixed rather than decisive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-08 71|News flow was mixed rather than one-way.|Sector trend does not fully support the thesis.
decision_reasoning: CSCO is the cleanest setup among the four but does not clear the standard recommend bar on contract quality. Trend is supportive (20d +10.8%, 50d +16.5%) with constructive market/sector tape (QQQ +4.1%, sector +6.4% 5d), and IV ~0.58 is reasonable. However, the only chain entries with workable liquidity require a ~7% move to break even, and CSCO historical earnings reactions are typically 4–6%. MP and IONQ both have 5/8 expiries against 5/7 earnings — essentially one day of post-print life, too tight for long premium. IONQ IV 1.14 is punitive. SPG is a long put (allowed under long_only) but thesis is weak: RS vs SPY only -6%, liquidity 35/20, no catalyst. CSCO on watchlist is the honest call.
